item.name: Auth
item.description: Registration creates users and stores a JWT in `access_token`. For existing users, run **Authenticate (BUYER)**, **Authenticate (SELLER)**, or **Authenticate (ADMIN)** (same emails/passwords as the Register examples).

request.description: POST `/auth/authenticate` as the buyer from **Register (BUYER)** (`buyer@test.com`). Saves JWT to collection variable `access_token` for Bearer auth.
POST `/auth/authenticate` as the seller from **Register (SELLER)** (`seller@test.com`). Saves JWT to `access_token`.
POST `/auth/authenticate` as the admin from **Register (ADMIN)** (`admin@test.com`). Saves JWT to `access_token`.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Requires ROLE_ADMIN. Use collection Bearer auth: run **Auth → Register (ADMIN)** or **Auth → Authenticate (ADMIN)** so `access_token` is set. Most calls need no body. Change `admin_user_id` for get/delete/reactivate by id. Filtered user lists: active, inactive, active buyers, active sellers.

request.description: Smoke test. Response: plain text, e.g. `Hi, Test Admin`.
All users with `active: true` (any role).
All users with `active: false`.
Users with role BUYER and `active: true`.
Users with role SELLER and `active: true`.
No body. Sets user `active` to false. Edit `admin_user_id` in collection variables (not the admin’s own id if you still need that session).
No body. Sets user `active` to true. May return error if user is already active (`UserAlreadyActivatedException`). Uses Bearer token explicitly (same as collection auth).
Authenticated user only. JWT identifies the account; body is only the new password.
Returns the authenticated user's profile (id, name, surname, email, telephone, registerDate, active, role). Requires Bearer token (collection auth).
All fields optional in the API (partial update); send only what you want to change.
No body. Requires Bearer token.

item.name: Sample
item.description: All routes require authentication (Bearer). GET: any authenticated user. POST/PUT/PATCH: SELLER (POST and PUT require the full sample body: price, stock, volumeMl, description, imageUrl, perfumeId; PATCH /{id}/stock updates only stock). DELETE: ADMIN or SELLER. Set `seller_id` and `perfume_id` to existing user/perfume IDs in your DB.

request.description: Returns active samples only.
Active samples for the given seller. May return 204 No Content if empty.
Active samples for the given perfume. 204 No Content if empty. 404 if the perfume does not exist.
404 if not found or soft-deleted (inactive).

Returns 201 Created with Location header. Body must include all fields: price, stock, volumeMl, description, imageUrl, perfumeId (400 if incomplete). Seller is taken from the JWT (Bearer); do not send sellerId in the body.

PUT replaces the sample with a full resource: send price, stock, volumeMl, description, imageUrl, and perfumeId (all required). 400 if any field is missing or blank. Requires Bearer (SELLER). You can only update samples you own (403 otherwise).
Updates only the stock of a sample. Requires Bearer (SELLER) and ownership (403 otherwise). Body: { "stock": >= 0 }. 400 if missing or negative.
Sets sample inactive. 204 No Content on success. SELLER may only delete their own samples; ADMIN may delete any.

item.name: Cart
item.description: Base **`/cart`**. **Bearer required.** All routes require **`ROLE_BUYER`** (use **Auth → Register (BUYER)** or login as buyer). Use **`{{sample_id}}`** for an existing active sample when adding lines. Set **`{{cart_item_id}}`** from **Add cart item** (test script saves `id` on 201) or from **Get cart** -> `items[].id`.

request.description: **200** + `CartResponse` (`id`, `items`, `totalPrice`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`). Pure read: if the buyer has **no cart row yet**, a synthetic empty response is returned (`items: []`, `totalPrice: 0`, without `id`/`createdAt`/`updatedAt`) and **nothing is persisted**. The cart row is created lazily on the first **POST /cart/items**.
**200** + `CartItemResponse` (`id`, `quantity`, `addedAt`, `sampleId`). **404** if the line is not in your cart.
**201 Created** + `CartItemResponse`; `Location: /cart/items/{id}`. Body: `sampleId` (required), `quantity` (required, >= 1). Duplicate `sampleId` merges quantities on the server.

**200 OK** + `CartItemResponse` with the updated line (`id`, `quantity`, `addedAt`, `sampleId`). Body: `quantity` (required, >= 1). **404** `CartItemNotFoundException` if the line is not yours. **404** `SampleNotFoundException` if the linked sample was **soft-deleted / inactive** after it was added (same rule as add: you cannot change quantity for inactive samples).

**204 No Content**. Deletes the line from your cart. If it was the **last** line, the cart row is also deleted (keeps the invariant `cart exists iff has items`). The next **GET /cart** will return the synthetic empty response; the next **POST /cart/items** lazily creates a fresh cart.

**204 No Content** - idempotent. Deletes all `CartItem` rows **and the `Cart` row itself**, so the post-state is identical to a buyer that never added anything (next **GET /cart** returns the synthetic empty response, next **POST /cart/items** lazily creates a fresh cart). If the buyer has no cart yet, this call is a no-op and still returns **204**.

**200 OK** + `OrderResponseDTO`. Converts the buyer's cart into an order: validates stock, decrements atomically, snapshots unit prices, persists order + items with status `PENDING`, then clears the cart. Additionally, creates the associated **Payment** (status `PENDING`, `methodName` null) and **Shipment** (status `PENDING`, `addressId` null, no tracking) for the order — the buyer is expected to fill those via `PATCH /payments/{id}/method` and `PATCH /shipments/{id}/address`. **400** `EmptyCartException` if the cart doesn't exist or has no items. **400** `InsufficientStockException` if any sample doesn't have enough stock. No body required.

item.name: Order
item.description: Order CRUD. Order **creation** is not exposed here - it is handled by the future `CartController#/cart/checkout`, which calls `OrderService#createOrder` internally. This folder only covers reads, admin status transitions, and buyer cancellation. Set `order_id` in collection variables before calling single-order endpoints.

request.description: Returns every order in the system. Requires ROLE_ADMIN. Log in as admin first via **Auth → Register (ADMIN)** or **Auth → Authenticate (ADMIN)** so `access_token` belongs to an admin.

Returns all orders belonging to the authenticated buyer (JWT subject). Any authenticated user can call this; admins see only their own orders via this endpoint - use **Get All Orders** for a full list.

Returns a single order. The service enforces ownership: a buyer can only read their own orders, while admins can read any. A non-owner buyer gets 403 `OrderAccessDeniedException`; a missing id gets 404 `OrderNotFoundException`. Edit `order_id` in collection variables.

Admin-only status transition. Allowed values: `PENDING`, `PAID`, `SHIPPED`, `DELIVERED`, `CANCELLED`. Anything else returns 400 `InvalidOrderStatusException`. Non-admins get 403 from SecurityConfig before reaching the controller.

Note: calling this with `CANCELLED` does NOT restore stock - use **Cancel Order** for proper cancellation of pending orders.

Cancels a PENDING order and restores each item's `Sample.stock`. No body. Buyer can only cancel their own order; admin can cancel any. Rules: order must exist (else 404 `OrderNotFoundException`), caller must own it or be admin (else 403 `OrderAccessDeniedException`), current status must be `PENDING` (else 400 `InvalidOrderStatusException`).

item.name: Review
item.description: Base **`/review`**. **Bearer required** for all routes. **GET** `/**`: any authenticated role. **POST** / **PUT**: **`ROLE_BUYER`** only. **DELETE**: **`ROLE_BUYER`** only, and only the owner of the review (the service enforces ownership). Body for **create**: `ReviewRequestDTO` (`sampleId` required, `rating` 1–5 required, `comment` optional, max 1000 chars). Body for **update**: `ReviewUpdateRequestDTO` (`rating` 1–5 required, `comment` optional, max 1000 chars; the sample/buyer cannot be changed on update — the review is identified by the path id). Blank/whitespace-only comments are normalized to `null`. List endpoints return `ReviewListResponseDTO` with `reviews`, `totalCount`, `averageRating` (always **200 OK**, even when empty). Set **`{{review_id}}`** from **Create review** (test script on 201) or from list responses; **`{{buyer_id}}`** for listing another buyer's reviews (same as a user's id in DB).

request.description: `GET /review/sample/{sampleId}`. **200 OK** + `ReviewListResponseDTO` (`reviews`, `totalCount`, `averageRating`). When the sample has no reviews yet, `reviews` is `[]`, `totalCount` is `0`, and `averageRating` is `null`. **404** `SampleNotFoundException` if the sample does not exist or is inactive.

`GET /review/buyer/{buyerId}`. **200 OK** + `ReviewListResponseDTO` with all reviews written by that buyer. Empty envelope (`reviews: []`, `totalCount: 0`, `averageRating: null`) if the buyer has no reviews.
`GET /review/seller/{sellerId}`. **200 OK** + `ReviewListResponseDTO` aggregating reviews across all samples that belong to the seller. Empty envelope if the seller has no reviewed samples.
`GET /review/{id}`. **200** + single `ReviewResponseDTO`. **404** if the review does not exist.

`POST /review`. **201 Created** + `ReviewResponseDTO`; `Location: /review/{id}`. Buyer is taken from the JWT. Body: `sampleId` (required), `rating` 1–5 (required), `comment` (optional, up to 1000 chars — a rating-only review is valid). **409** `ReviewAlreadyExistsException` if the buyer already reviewed that sample (enforced by DB unique constraint on `(buyer_id, sample_id)`). **400** for invalid rating or oversized comment. **404** if the sample does not exist.

`PUT /review/{id}`. **200 OK** + updated `ReviewResponseDTO`. Body: `ReviewUpdateRequestDTO` (`rating` 1–5 required, `comment` optional up to 1000 chars). **The review's sample and buyer cannot be changed** — the review is identified by the path id, so `sampleId` is not part of the update body. Only the **owner** buyer may update; otherwise the server responds with the ownership error. **400** for invalid rating or oversized comment. **404** if the review does not exist.

`DELETE /review/{id}`. **200 OK** + plain text confirmation. **Only the owner buyer** may delete their own review: `SecurityConfig` restricts this route to `ROLE_BUYER`, and the service throws the ownership error if the caller isn't the author. ADMINs cannot delete reviews. **404** if the review does not exist.

item.name: Address
item.description:
Base **`/address`**. Bearer token required. **GET** (`/address/...`): **BUYER** or **ADMIN**. **POST** / **PUT** / **DELETE**: **BUYER** only. A buyer can have **many** addresses (`User` 1—N `Address`). **Delete** is a **soft delete** (`active = false`): the row stays so **Shipment** and other FKs are not broken. Attempting to delete an already-inactive address returns **409 Conflict**.

Use collection variable **`{{address_id}}`** (set it from **Add address** or **List addresses** response `id`).

request.description: `POST /address/addAddress`. Body: `CreateAddressRequest`. **201** + `AddressResponse` (`id`, `active`, …). You can add multiple addresses per user.
`GET /address/addresses`. **200** + JSON array of **active** `AddressResponse` (empty array if none).

`GET /address/{addressId}`. **200** + single `AddressResponse` (active or inactive) for the authenticated buyer. **404** (`AddressNotFoundException`) if the id does not exist or does not belong to you.
`PUT /address/modifyAddress/{addressId}`. Body: `CreateAddressRequest`. **200** + `AddressResponse`. **404** if the id does not exist, is not yours, or is already soft-deleted.

`DELETE /address/deleteAddress/{addressId}`. **Soft delete** only (`active` → false); row remains for FK integrity. **200** + `Address successfully deleted`. **404** if id missing or not owned by you. **409 Conflict** (`AddressAlreadyInactiveException`) if the address is already inactive — a second delete is **not** a no-op.

`GET /payments/{id}` (`PaymentController#getById`). Returns **200** + `PaymentResponse`. Accessible to **ADMIN** or the **BUYER** that owns the underlying order (`403` `PaymentForbiddenException` otherwise). `404` `PaymentNotFoundException` if the id doesn't exist.

`GET /payments/order/{orderId}` (`PaymentController#getByOrderId`). Returns **200** + `PaymentResponse` for the payment linked to that order. Same access rules as **Get payment by id**. `404` if no payment exists for the order (should not happen after a successful checkout).

`PATCH /payments/{id}/method` (`PaymentController#updateMethod`). Body: `{ "methodName": "..." }` (non-blank). Sets the payment method chosen by the buyer after `Checkout` (payment is created with `methodName = null`). Only allowed while the payment is **PENDING** — `409` `PaymentNotPendingException` once it transitions to `COMPLETED` / `FAILED` / `REFUNDED`. **200** + updated `PaymentResponse`. **400** on blank body. **403** for non-owners; **404** if the payment id doesn't exist.

item.name: Shipments
item.description: Base **`/shipments`** (`ShipmentController`). Bearer **`{{access_token}}`** (collection auth). Shipments are **auto-created by Checkout** with `addressId = null` and `status = PENDING` — there is no manual create endpoint. **GET**: **ADMIN**, **BUYER** (order buyer), or **SELLER** (all order line samples belong to that seller). **PATCH …/address**: **ADMIN** or **BUYER** (order owner) — only while **PENDING**. **PATCH …/status** and **PATCH …/tracking**: **ADMIN** or **SELLER** (same seller rule). **PATCH …/tracking** sets **`trackingNumber` once** (immutable afterward). `status` in JSON is a string enum: `PENDING`, `SHIPPED`, `IN_TRANSIT`, `DELIVERED`, `RETURNED`. Set **`{{shipment_id}}`** from **Get shipment by order id** after Checkout.

request.description: `GET /shipments/{id}`. **200** + `ShipmentResponse` (`id`, `shippedAt`, `deliveredAt`, `status`, `trackingNumber`, `orderId`, `addressId`). **404** if no shipment. **403** if the token user may not view this shipment.
`GET /shipments/order/{orderId}`. **200** + `ShipmentResponse` for that order. **404** if the order has no shipment yet. **403** if not allowed to view.

`PATCH /shipments/{id}/address` (`ShipmentController#updateAddress`). Body: `{ "addressId": N }` (required). Sets the shipping address — used by the buyer after `Checkout` because the shipment is created with `addressId = null`. The address **must belong to the current buyer** and be active (`404` `AddressNotFoundException` otherwise). Only allowed while the shipment is **PENDING** — `409` `ShipmentNotPendingException` once it leaves PENDING. **200** + updated `ShipmentResponse`. **403** for non-owner buyers / sellers; **404** if the shipment id doesn't exist.

`PATCH /shipments/{id}/status`. Body: `{ "status": "PENDING" | "SHIPPED" | "IN_TRANSIT" | "DELIVERED" | "RETURNED" }`. **200** + updated `ShipmentResponse`. If the shipment is still **PENDING**, you must set a non-blank **trackingNumber** first (**Set shipment tracking (once)** or on create) before moving to any other status — else **400** `ShipmentTrackingRequiredException`. **404** / **403** as in service rules.

`PATCH /shipments/{id}/tracking` (`ShipmentController#setTrackingNumber`). Body: `{ "trackingNumber": "..." }` (non-blank). Sets the tracking **only if it is still unset** (null/blank); once stored it **cannot be changed** — second call returns **409** `ShipmentTrackingImmutableException`. **200** + `ShipmentResponse`.
